<parameter=code>

    p = n['pixels']

        center_c = sum([pt[1] for pt in b]) // len(b)
        print(f"  {c} p={p} ({center_r},{center_c}) h={n['hash'][:8]}")

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
So clicking sky blue at (24,37) changed G(23,35) back to W(23,35). So it reversed the previous orange click effect. The pattern seems to be:
- Click S → shifts objects or reverses the last change
Let me think about the state transitions more carefully:
State A (after step 22): G(6,17), O(13,25), W(23,35), S(24,37), G(35,45), p(41,49), G(48,54)
Click O → State B: G(6,17), O(13,25), G(23,35), S(24,37), G(35,45), p(41,49), G(48,54) [W→G]
Click S → State A: G(6,17), O(13,25), W(23,35), S(24,37), G(35,45), p(41,49), G(48,54) [G→W]
So clicking S reverses the effect of clicking O. And clicking O on state A changes W to G.
This seems like a toggle: O toggles W↔G, and S reverses the last action.
Wait, but earlier clicking S caused a shift (orange moved from 19,30 to 13,25). Let me check if there's a pattern based on the position of S.
Actually, I think I need to step back and think about the overall goal. Let me look at the initial state of Level 2 again and see what the "solved" state might look like.
Initial Level 2 state (from transition 13):
- W (6,17) - white diamond
- G (9,49) - dark gray square  
- O (12,24) - orange square
- G (21,8) - dark gray square (this was on the top chain)
- Chain segments: w(10,40), w(11,32)
- Bottom: G(35,45), p(41,49), G(48,54)
- Purple ring (top right)
- Orange ring (bottom)
The goal might be to get all objects to be dark gray (G), or to get the colored objects (orange and purple) to their matching rings.
Let me try a different approach. Let me see what happens if I click the purple square again from the current state.
